persona_id: 3291.description: Month-over-month change in total card spending; indicates spending growth or decline.
Average monetary amount per transaction; helps distinguish between many small purchases vs. few large ones.
Most frequent Merchant Category Codes (e.g., groceries, travel); reveals spending priorities.
Breakdown of transaction currencies; shows multi-currency usage.
Share of transactions where merchant country differs from cardholder's country.
Average spend per weekday; identifies weekday vs. weekend patterns.
Number of transactions exceeding a predefined high-value threshold.
Identifies regular subscription or installment payments.
How often the customer withdraws cash using the card.
Number of times spending exceeded the credit limit.
Percentage of payments made by the due date.
Average number of days a payment is delayed when past due.
Tendency to pay only the minimum amount due each cycle.
Percentage of total balance paid off each month.
How long the account has been active; older accounts indicate stability.
Flags accounts with no transaction activity over a long period.
Proportion of transactions made with an expired card (often fraud indicator).
Comparison of spending within home country vs. abroad.
Countries where the customer spends the most.
Risk from holding or transacting in multiple foreign currencies.
Degree to which spending is concentrated in one or few locations.
How often the customer makes transactions while traveling.
Detects spending behavior that deviates from historical norms.
Tracks transactions in high-risk merchant categories (e.g., gambling, adult entertainment).
Sudden spikes in spending over a short time window.
Transactions occurring outside normal business hours.
Rate of transactions per unit of time (e.g., per hour or per day).
Customers with consistently high spending or profitability.
Compares new accounts to long-standing ones for risk/behavior differences.
Variety of merchant categories or transaction types used.
Month-over-month percentage growth in total spending.
Recurring spending peaks tied to holidays, seasons, or annual events.
Change in the number of transactions over time.
Projected total value a customer will generate over the entire relationship.
Likelihood that the customer will close the account or stop using the product.
Overall net profit generated by the customer after costs and risk.
Similar to monthly_spending_trend; tracks total spend changes month to month.
Average transaction amount; helps segment customers by purchase size.
How transactions are initiated (e.g., chip, swipe, manual key entry).
Top merchant category codes by spend volume.
Spending patterns segmented by day of the week.
Spending patterns by calendar day (e.g., 1st to 31st).
Count of transactions above a high-value threshold.
Identifies fixed periodic payments (subscriptions, bills).
How much spending is concentrated in specific cities.
Number of transactions per time period (daily/weekly/monthly).
Upward or downward trend in overall account usage over time.
Length and frequency of inactive periods without transactions.
Rate of transaction activity, often used for fraud detection.
Compares transaction volume between weekdays and weekends.
Total number of transactions per month.
Spending trends analyzed on a quarterly basis.
Spending variations tied to seasons or specific annual periods.
Statistical spread of transaction amounts (e.g., small vs. large).
Average amount spent per calendar day.
Ratio of local to cross-border spending.
Cities where the customer spends the most money.
Spread of transaction locations across different regions.
Transactions where merchant and cardholder are in different countries.
Breakdown of spend by the merchant's country.
How much spending occurs with merchants in the cardholder's home area.
Spending behavior analyzed per city.
Transactions spread across multiple cities in a short period.
Spending breakdown by merchant category type.
Merchants where the customer spends the most money.
Number of unique merchants visited over a period.
Regular, repeated transactions with the same merchant.
Rate at which the customer tries new merchants.
Focus of spending on a specific merchant type or vertical.
Spending at high-end or luxury merchants.
Ratio of essential (e.g., groceries, utilities) to discretionary (e.g., dining, travel) spending.
Comparison of e-commerce vs. physical store transactions.
Anomalous behavior not matching the customer's typical profile.
Tracks spend in high-risk merchant category codes.
Transactions made during late night or early morning hours.
Multiple transactions occurring in very quick succession.
Locations that are unusual given the customer's history.
Transaction amounts that deviate significantly from normal range.
Rate of transactions as a fraud or risk indicator.
Risk level associated with how a transaction was entered (e.g., manual vs. chip).
Spending behavior during major holiday periods.
Overall composite risk score for the customer.
Total amount of money transferred (in/out) per month.
Average monetary value of each transfer.
Number of transfers above a large-value threshold.
Number of transfers per weekday.
Hours of the day when transfer activity is highest.
Spread of transfer amounts (small vs. large).
Whether the customer is a net sender or receiver of funds.
Number of transfers per time period.
How much total transfer value is concentrated in few transactions.
Breakdown by transfer type (e.g., ACH, wire, internal).
Value concentration by specific transfer type.
How often each transfer type is used.
Transfer types that typically carry high values.
Regular, repeating transfers (e.g., payroll, rent).
Changes in usage of different transfer types over time.
Seasonal patterns in specific transfer types.
Risk flags associated with particular transfer types.
Ratio of transfers within the same bank vs. to external banks.
Total value of transfers between the customer's own accounts.
Total value of transfers to accounts at other banks.
Counterparties the customer transacts with most often.
How much total value is concentrated with few counterparties.
Analysis of external beneficiaries receiving funds.
Transfers that cross international borders.
Ratio of domestic to international transfers.
Transfer concentration in specific geographic regions.
Trend in outgoing (debit) transfer activity over time.
Change in net inflows vs. outflows over time.
Groups customers by similar transaction behavior patterns.
Categorizes customers into segments based on behavior or value.
Predicted total value a customer will bring over the entire relationship.
Probability that the customer will stop using the product.
Number and variety of products the customer holds.
How many of the available products the customer uses.
Composite measure of how actively the customer uses the account.
Transfer activity that deviates from the customer's normal behavior.
Sudden increases in transfer amounts over a short period.
Transfer activity occurring outside normal business hours.
Transactions with counterparties flagged as high risk.
Risk score related to regulatory compliance violations.
Signals that may indicate anti-money laundering concerns.
Likelihood that the customer is involved in fraudulent activity.
Flags related to operational issues (e.g., failed transactions).
Risk from over-reliance on a single product, channel, or counterparty.
Aggregate risk score combining multiple risk dimensions.
Total number of Certificate of Deposit (CD) accounts held.
Total principal amount invested in CDs.
Average term length of CD investments.
Expected interest income from CDs over their lifetime.
Proportion of CDs that are currently active vs. matured.
Concentration of CD investments in a single institution or tenor.
Total number of individual CD positions.
Month-over-month growth in CD holdings.
Foreign currency exposure within CD investments.
Risk that maturing CDs will be reinvested at lower rates.
How quickly CD investments can be converted to cash without penalty.
Total number of distinct products held by the customer.
Variety of product types across the customer's portfolio.
Sum of principal across all investment and deposit products.
Currency breakdown across all products.
Net interest generated after costs and provisions.
Difference between short-term assets and liabilities.
Length of time the customer has been with the bank.
Overall profit generated from the customer relationship.
Number of products and services used relative to available offerings.
Measures the customer's ability to meet short-term cash needs.
